items.title: From celebration to calls of weakness, how the Trump-pope relationship soured

children.content: US President Donald Trump described Pope Leo XIV's accession to the papacy as a meaningful moment for America. But now he has said the pontiff is hurting the Catholic Church, and is weak. Here's how their relationship has developed over the last year.

gallery.altText: A graphic photo of both Donald Trump and Pope Leo.

thumbnail.altText: A graphic photo of both Donald Trump and Pope Leo.

items.title: Why clearing mines from Strait of Hormuz might prove tricky for US

children.content: The prospect of mines in the Strait of Hormuz has highlighted a gap in US naval capabilities and reinforced its need for NATO help.

image.altText: A ship sails with others in the Gulf of Oman.

items.title: Japan, Australia sign deal for production of first three frigates

children.content: Defence Minister Richard Marles and his Japanese counterpart, Koizumi Shinjirō, signed the deal aboard one of the warships docked in Melbourne.

image.altText: Richard Marles and Koizumi Shinjiro sit side by side onboard a warship signing documents.

items.title: Who's really behind 'Big Australia'? Taylor's high immigration stance raises eyebrows

children.content: Angus Taylor's criticism of high immigration puts a strange spotlight on his own party's policy legacy.

image.altText: Angus Taylor stands in the courtyard of Parliament House with a TV camera pointed at him.

items.title: 'It's a free-for-all': Fiji 'ground zero' for transnational drug-smuggling trade

children.content: Two men have told Foreign Correspondent how they were unwittingly drawn into Fiji's largest-ever meth importation.

image.altText: A marina from above.

items.title: A family mystery in Italy helped me unravel the myth of the perfect mother

children.content: I traced my family’s maternal line from a home for abandoned children in Italy to my own wheelchair, confronting the myths we cling to about what makes a good mother — and the damage they do.

gallery.altText: Silhouette of a mother and young child facing away from each other with an image of an old Italian city in the background

thumbnail.altText: Silhouette of a mother and young child facing away from each other with an image of an old Italian city in the background

items.title: Eight barges floating in the sea are the heart of Japan's oil reserve

children.content: Japan has oil stockpile sites littered around the country, and as the world faces a fuel crisis, neighbours in Asia have been turning to Tokyo for assistance.

image.altText: An aerial view shows eight barges floating in the water just off Japan.

items.title: Crackdown on unregistered NDIS providers coming in bid to save $6b a year

children.content: NDIS Minister Mark Butler will announce reforms to the disability scheme on Wednesday that will aim to save more than $6 billion a year by 2035 — and the largely unregulated market of NDIS providers is at the top of his reform list.

image.altText: A tent with letters which read N D I S stands tall outside with trees in the background.

items.title: The mystery of an outback 'catch and kill' squad during WWII has been solved

children.content: In the 1940s, a shadowy military unit was assembled to catch and kill Japanese soldiers on Australia's remote northern coast. The identity of the so-called Solomons Six was a mystery — until now.

gallery.altText: Four back and white headshots of men dressed in army uniform.

thumbnail.altText: Four back and white headshots of men dressed in army uniform.

items.title: Iran opens fire on ships travelling through Strait of Hormuz

children.content: The two Indian-flagged vessels reported being fired upon by gunboats from Iran's Islamic Revolutionary Guards Corps, just a day after the strait was declared open by Iran's foreign minister. Follow live.

image.altText: A number of ships and tankers float in the sea beyond a rocky outcrop.

items.title: The quiet disappearance of Australia's rainfall observation network

children.content: For more than 60 years, Frank Pritchard has diligently checked the weather, adding to a record that stretches back more than a century. But he's part of a quietly shrinking network of observers around Australia. Without it, scientists say we risk missing out on important information that could help improve weather forecast models and climate projections.

image.altText: An older man with balding white hair holds up a rain gauge.

items.title: Why Australian tea tree oil could be banned in Europe

children.content: The EU is considering banning Australian tea tree oil after a study raised concerns about potential reproductive risks, a move industry leaders warn could devastate exports and trigger global impacts.

image.altText: A bottle of tea tree solution from brand Eureka.

items.title: Car hits pedestrians in Melbourne, killing Supernova Comic Con attendee

children.content: A young man who was killed when a car struck a group of pedestrians in Melbourne's north-west was on his way home from attending the Supernova Comic Con & Gaming convention, police say.

image.altText: A smashed car behind police tape.

items.title: 'She was us': History behind Joy, the first sex worker statue

children.content: During her short residency in Sydney, the statue of sex worker Joy had many detractors and was even vandalised. Thirty years later, she has returned, with her supporters hoping she will dismantle stereotypes that still plague the industry.

items.title: There's a confidence in you I admire. Let's play, HARD!

children.content: Righteo champ, it's time to put down your toast and impress with your trivia prowess. Let's play, HARD!

image.altText: Tom Gleeson on the set of Hard Quiz gesturing behind him with a serious expression.

items.title: Disco diva and hit podcaster Jessie Ware might be music’s coolest mum

children.content: Whether making opulent, grown-up disco-pop or doing school pick-up, the glam British artist is having more fun than ever.

image.altText: Middle-aged brunette woman wearing fashionable skirt, bra & chain top poses with right hand upright

items.title: The cast of Good News Week reflect on 30 years of the comedy game show

children.content: On April 19, 1999, comedy news game show Good News Week premiered, hosted by Paul McDermott, sparking nine seasons, a Gold Logie nomination and some first-chances for comedians.

gallery.altText: Paul McDermott, 30s, Julie McCrossin, 40s, and Mikey Robins, 30s, pose together on a stage littered with papers.

thumbnail.altText: Paul McDermott, 30s, Julie McCrossin, 40s, and Mikey Robins, 30s, pose together on a stage littered with papers.

avatar.alt: Hannah Story, a blonde woman sitting outside smiles. A dog is at her feet, and she wears a T-shirt that reads "MIRANDA JULY".

items.title: 'Humble' Buddy praised on return to country footy roots

children.content: Lance Franklin has pulled on the boots to help the Dowerin Tigers, where he played his formative footy, raise much-needed funds for lights and facilities.

gallery.altText: Lance Franklin runs out with his Dowerin teammates for the game against Gingin.
A football team of boys in striped jumpers, including AFL star Buddy Franklin, wearing medals from a win in 1997

items.title: Strait of Hormuz closes as US blockade continues: Iranian official — as it happened

children.content: Iran has labelled the ongoing US blockade of the strait as a violation of the ceasefire agreement, while Donald Trump says "very good conversations are going on" between the two countries.

image.altText: US President Donald Trump gives a thumbs up.

items.title: Indemnified soldiers give evidence in war veteran's case, documents show

children.content: Ben Roberts-Smith was on Friday granted bail in the NSW Local Court, with his case over war crimes charges expected to return to court in early June.

image.altText: Ben Roberts Smith in a blue suit, white shirt, outside court on a sunny day.

items.title: Crows sensationally down Saints after emotional scenes at Adelaide Oval

children.content: The Crows hold on by one point in a thriller at Adelaide Oval, as the club pays tribute to grieving skipper Jordan Dawson.

image.altText: Two AFL players in stripes hug and celebrate a goal

items.title: Protesters arrested at pro-Palestine rally in Brisbane

children.content: Protesters have been arrested for chanting and displaying a phrase banned in Queensland at a rally in Brisbane on Saturday.

image.altText: A woman in white being arrested

items.title: World's largest democracy votes against increasing women

children.content: The largest democracy in the world has rejected a move to increase the number of women in parliament. What could have been a 'beacon' for global women's rights became marred by controversy.

gallery.altText: Four women wearing saris smile for a camera and hold up the peace sign with their hands.

thumbnail.altText: Four women wearing saris smile for a camera and hold up the peace sign with their hands.

items.title: Triple murderer Erin Patterson's estranged husband writing his memoir

children.content: Simon Patterson, who claims his murderous ex-wife tried to poison him on several occasions, is pursuing a book deal, his media manager has announced.

image.altText: Simon and Erin Patterson in a photo graphic in front of Ms Patterson's Leongatha home.

items.title: Haas expected to miss several weeks as Broncos count cost of brave win

children.content: Brisbane's horror run of injuries has continued with Payne Haas limping off in the first half of the Broncos' one-point win over Wests Tigers.

image.altText: A rugby league player in dark blue walks off the field looking down accompanied by a man in orange

items.title: The world faces a grim reality, even if Trump exists in a different one

children.content: The world is stuck watching a performative series of announcements about meetings that could, may, will happen, involving a president not dealing with the reality that he is in a weak and confused bargaining position.

gallery.altText: Donald Trump stands outside the White House as reporters hold microphones.

thumbnail.altText: Donald Trump stands outside the White House as reporters hold microphones.

items.title: Gout celebrates early to win another title, says magic mark 'coming soon'

children.content: Gout Gout waves goodbye to his opponents en route to another national under-20 title in the 100m, as the wait goes on for him to dip below the 10-second barrier.

image.altText: Gout Gout waves at an opponent in the national under-20 100m final.

items.title: Wallaroos take tornado 'core memories' but no wins away from series in US

children.content: Australia loses 33-12 to the USA Eagles in their Pacific Four Series after storms in Kansas City delay kick-off by almost three hours.

image.altText: USA Eagles player Cassidy Bargell fends off Australian Wallaroos player Eva Karpani.

items.title: Skip the takeaway. This halloumi wrap is quicker and healthy too

children.content: The only cooking in this simple dinner is the halloumi. It has takeaway vibes and just the right balance of flavours to become a family favourite.

gallery.altText: A round flatbread on a kitchen bench with salad and grilled halloumi filling in the centre.

thumbnail.altText: A round flatbread on a kitchen bench with salad and grilled halloumi filling in the centre.

items.title: Old is new again: Gen alpha takes to old tech with fresh eyes

children.content: With the constant evolution of the digital world, community history is rapidly disappearing into memories and museums. But a historical society on WA's south coast is delighting today's kids by showing them the technology older Australians grew up with.

image.altText: A young girl looks into the rear of a bellows camera

children.content: Video of an off-duty police officer restraining a 13yo girl on a Perth train has sparked accusations of excessive force.

image.altText: A young girl holds her head and winces with her face being blurred.

children.content: Carnival Cruise Line has confirmed it is assisting authorities in a search for a missing man.

children.content: Tech companies are coming under fire as Australian parents increasingly voice concerns that some online platforms are failing to keep kids under 16 off their platforms.

children.content: NSW Police has expressed concern about escalating violence between Sydney's organised crime networks, following several alleged arson attacks and public shootings overnight.

items.title: Harry and Meghan seek duty and dollars on tour

children.content: Mixing philanthropy, influencing and brand building, Harry and Meghan’s Australian tour revives a question Queen Elizabeth once shut down: can the 'half in, half out' royal life work?
